generator.determinism: No wall-clock timestamp is stored; concrete Z3 model values are not used as core facts.
generator.source_commit_policy: Best-effort commit at generation time; mapping and schema validation are the stable comparison keys.

representative_rendered_expressions.error:
Found 1 errors during parsing:
Error 1: Syntax error at line 1, column 0: Invalid character or token

overrides.assign:
{{ target }} = self._evaluate_runtime_expr(
    lambda: {{ expr }},
    {{ ("operation assignment to '" ~ name ~ "'") | tojson }},
)

stmt_overrides.assign:
{{ target }} = self._evaluate_runtime_expr(
    lambda: {{ expr }},
    {{ ("operation assignment to '" ~ name ~ "'") | tojson }},
)

division_modulo_cases.note: Python renderer uses true division for '/' expressions.
Python modulo keeps the divisor sign.

representative_notes.summary: Python samples are a P3 infinite-precision simulation-compatible baseline, not the fixed-width default profile.
Z3 arithmetic division and modulo are total at zero; later solver work must carry definedness obligations.
Current solver helpers raise NotImplementedError for trigonometric, hyperbolic, logarithmic and exponential functions.
The parser currently rejects '~A' in numeric expressions. The Z3 samples also record TypeError for Int bitwise construction, while BitVec provides fixed-width bitwise operations.

z3.seed_policy: Capability rows avoid solver model dependence; future model-valued rows must record a fixed seed.

render_paths.note: No specialized BinaryOp(+) entry exists; the generic renderer emits the AST operator token.

BitVec.note: Exact for a selected bit width; overflow wraps by BitVec semantics.

FP.note: Exact for chosen FP sort and rounding mode, not Python int semantics.

Int.note: Exact over mathematical integers.
Int.strategy: native arithmetic

Real.note: Exact over mathematical reals.

render_paths.note: No specialized BinaryOp(/) entry exists; the generic renderer emits the AST operator token.

BitVec.note: BitVec division is width-bound and needs a signedness profile.
BitVec.strategy: signed/unsigned operator must be chosen explicitly

FP.note: FP division follows selected rounding/NaN/Inf semantics, not Python integers.

Int.note: Z3 Int division is integer-valued and totalized at divisor zero; it is not exact for the Python renderer's true-division baseline.
Int.strategy: integer division with definedness and Python-compatibility obligations

Real.note: Z3 Real division is totalized at zero; probes must record non-zero obligations.

render_paths.note: No specialized BinaryOp(%) entry exists; the generic renderer emits the AST operator token.

BitVec.note: BitVec remainder is width-bound and needs a signedness profile.
BitVec.strategy: signed/unsigned remainder operator must be chosen explicitly

FP.note: FP remainder is outside current solver helper coverage.

Int.note: Z3 Int modulo is totalized at divisor zero and does not match Python modulo when the divisor is negative.
Int.strategy: integer modulo with divisor-sign and definedness obligations

Real.note: Real modulo is not a native Z3 arithmetic operator.

render_paths.note: No specialized BinaryOp(**) entry exists; the generic renderer emits the AST operator token.

BitVec.note: No generic FCSTM BitVec power profile is defined.

FP.note: No FP power encoding is used by current solver helpers.

Int.note: Nonlinear/exponential constraints can be incomplete or slow.
Int.strategy: native exponent where accepted by z3py

Real.note: General real exponentiation is not a stable exact solver baseline.
Real.strategy: native exponent where accepted by z3py
